content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Heating". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/problemset/problem/1260/A
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then T test cases follow. The first line of each test case contains two integers n and m. The second line contains n integers representing the temperatures in the rooms.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the minimum number of heaters needed to heat all the rooms.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Sort the rooms and heaters by their temperatures, then use two pointers to find the minimum number of heaters needed.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n log n + m log m)

code.Python3:
import sys
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    n, m = map(int, input().split())
    rooms = list(map(int, input().split()))
    heaters = list(map(int, input().split()))
    rooms.sort()
    heaters.sort()
    i, j, count = 0, 0, 0
    while i < n:
        while j < m and heaters[j] < rooms[i]:
            j += 1
        if j == m:
            break
        count += 1
        i += 1
        j += 1
    print(count)
